variables.name: net_income
variables.description: Income after cost, deposits, rent, platform fees, and time cost.

variables.name: compliance_risk
variables.description: License, platform rule, contract, and work constraint risk.

variables.name: health_load
variables.description: Sleep, physical load, accident risk, and long-term sustainability.

variables.name: maintenance_cost
variables.description: Learning, equipment, deposits, accounts, and time management.

variables.name: reversibility
variables.description: Ability to exit after trial without high sunk cost.

assumptions: Prefer low maintenance, low cost, and low legal risk.
Do not assume high deposit, vehicle purchase, or long-term heavy labor is acceptable unless explicitly allowed.
Version one uses structural modeling first and does not disguise language reasoning as strict numerical simulation.
Hard avoid constraint: large vehicle purchase.
Hard avoid constraint: long daytime work after night shift.
Hard avoid constraint: high accident exposure.
Hard avoid constraint: unclear platform penalties.
causal_chain: Environment and constraints define the option space.
Resources, cost, compliance, and physical load shape net return and sustainability.
High-uncertainty variables require warning indicators and stop-loss rules.
The final path should prefer low legal risk, low sunk cost, and reversible testing.

scenario_tree.base_case: Use conservative assumptions and validate with a small trial.
scenario_tree.upside_case: If key variables improve, scale only after evidence is collected.
scenario_tree.downside_case: If return, compliance, or health variables worsen, trigger stop-loss.

risk_factors.control: Require red-team objections and disconfirming variables.
Include deposits, equipment, commute, and sleep loss in total cost.
Expose uncertainty instead of pretending certainty.

decision_options.description: Keep the current path as a low-risk baseline.
Run a low-deposit, short-cycle, reversible test.
Reject high-deposit, illegal, or sleep-damaging routes.

mesa_recommendation.reason: Delivery and ride-hailing earnings depend on many workers competing for limited orders and time windows.

uncertainties: Without real cost and demand data, the result is structured judgment rather than strict numerical simulation.
External model output is evidence or review input, not final truth.

mesa_result.reason: Delivery and ride-hailing earnings depend on many workers competing for limited orders and time windows.

summary.interpretation: With limited demand, returns can diverge under competition and random allocation.

warnings: This is a first-version minimal Mesa-compatible runner, not an industry-grade simulation.

planner.summary: For a night-shift security guard in Fuzhou, the baseline security job is the most sustainable for low upfront cost, low fatigue, low accident exposure, low exit cost, and low maintenance. Among side options, food delivery ranks highest due to low entry cost and reversible trial, but all side options increase fatigue and accident risk. A one-week trial with strict stop conditions is recommended before switching.

risks.ride_hailing: High upfront/vehicle cost, high deposit, high accident exposure, irreversible commitment, platform penalties unclear.
risks.food_delivery: Moderate fatigue from daytime riding after night shift, moderate accident exposure, low exit cost.
risks.pupu_grocery_delivery: Similar to food delivery but may involve heavier loads and longer distances, increasing fatigue and accident risk.
risks.security_guard_baseline: Low net income growth, but no additional risks; safest choice for constraints.

planner.recommendation: Stick with security guard baseline. If side income is desired, attempt a one-week food delivery trial only if day sleep is feasible and accident insurance is purchased. Stop immediately if fatigue affects main job or expenses exceed earnings.

modeler.summary: For a night-shift security guard in Fuzhou, the baseline security job is most sustainable due to no upfront cost, low fatigue, low accident exposure, low exit cost, and minimal maintenance. Among side options, food delivery ranks highest for low entry cost and reversibility, but all side jobs increase fatigue and accident risk. A one-week food delivery trial with strict stop conditions is the only recommended side income path, provided day sleep and accident insurance are feasible.

risks.security_guard_baseline: Low net income growth, no additional risks; safest choice under constraints.
risks.ride_hailing: High upfront/vehicle cost, high deposit, high accident exposure, irreversible commitment, unclear platform penalties, increased fatigue from double shifts.
risks.food_delivery: Moderate fatigue from daytime riding after night shift, moderate accident exposure, low exit cost, requires reliable scooter or e-bike.
risks.pupu_grocery_delivery: Similar to food delivery but heavier loads and longer distances increase fatigue and accident risk; may require vehicle with storage capacity.

modeler.recommendation: Stick with security guard baseline. If side income is essential, attempt a one-week food delivery trial only if: (1) 6-7 hours of uninterrupted day sleep is guaranteed, (2) accident insurance is purchased, (3) upfront scooter rental is ≤500 RMB deposit. Stop immediately if fatigue impairs security job performance, total expenses exceed earnings, or accident occurs.
